title: Triphysaria pusilla
intro: Triphysaria pusilla, also known as dwarf wildflower or tiny shootingstar, is a small, annual or perennial plant native to the western United States. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Triphysaria pusilla include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Triphysaria pusilla is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in topical creams and ointments for skin issues and wound care.
taxonomy: Triphysaria pusilla is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. The genus Triphysaria is a group of annual and perennial herbs in the plant family Scrophulariaceae, commonly known as figworts. This genus is closely related to other genera such as Castilleja and Orthocarpus. Triphysaria pusilla is further classified as belonging to the section Triphysaria in the tribe Scrophularieae. The classification of Triphysaria pusilla reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Scrophulariaceae family.
